Plaintiff Bethania Bacigalupe?S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Attorney Lien, Or In The Alternative An Order Requiring Former Counsel To Attend Mandatory Settlement Conference
Off Calendar Jun-15-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Tenny Mirzayan, Esq. KELLER FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP, 818.342.7442, for Plaintiff Bethania Bacigalupe.
Karen Matcke Crosby, Esq. (925) 756-6062, prior counsel to plaintiff Bethania Bacigalupe, Specially appearing on her own behalf.

No argument is held. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BETHANIA BACIGALUPE'S Motion For Relief From Attorney Lien, Or In The Alternative An Order Requiring Former Counsel To Attend Mandatory Settlement Conference.

Plaintiff's "motion for an order setting aside defective attorney lien or, in the alternative an order requiring former counsel to attend mandatory settlement conference" is denied.

Both sides rely on Carroll v. Interstate Brands Corp. (2002) 99 Cal.App.4th 1168. It holds that "the trial court in the underlying action [e.g., this action] has no jurisdiction to determine the existence or validity of an attorney's lien on the judgment." (Id. at 1173.) Rather, a "separate, independent action" must be brought. (Id.) Further, under this court's local rules, mandatory settlement conferences are not within this department's domain.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)

Motion For An Order Enforcing Settlement
Off Calendar Nov-16-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BETHANIA BACIGALUPE's Motion For An Order Enforcing Settlement pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. section 664.6 is denied. Code Civ. Proc. section 664.6(a) provides that the court may enter judgment pursuant to the terms of a settlement agreement if the parties pending litigation entered into "a writing signed by the parties outside the presence of the court or orally before the court." This motion is premature as plaintiff fails to proffer either a written or oral settlement in conformance with the statute. Moreover, a term of the settlement was that plaintiff would release her rights, which has yet to occur. (Welch Decl., par. 7.)

Counsel for Defendant to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
